data.content:
Here's my analysis of the two custom targets:

---

**Target 1: `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)** — 🔴 Always re-runs + always dirties downstream

- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → MSBuild runs it unconditionally every build.
- Uses `DateTime.Now` → the output file content changes every time, even when nothing else changed.
- While `build-timestamp.txt` isn't compiled, the target always executes needlessly.

**Target 2: `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)** — 🔴 Always re-runs + invalidates CoreCompile

- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → runs unconditionally every build.
- `WriteLinesToFile ... Overwrite="true"` **updates the file's last-write timestamp** even when the git hash hasn't changed.
- That file is added to `<Compile>` → CoreCompile sees a "changed" input → **full recompilation on every build**.

---

Let me apply fixes:

arguments.old_str:
<Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
      <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
    </Exec>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                     Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                     Overwrite="true" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Only regenerate the timestamp when source files or the project file change -->
  <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
          Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(Compile)"
          Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt"
                     Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())"
                     Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Always resolve the git hash, but only rewrite the file when it actually changes
       so the unchanged timestamp doesn't invalidate CoreCompile -->
  <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
      <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
    </Exec>
    <PropertyGroup>
      <_GitHashFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs</_GitHashFile>
      <_GitHashContent>namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }</_GitHashContent>
      <_ExistingGitHash Condition="Exists('$(_GitHashFile)')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('$(_GitHashFile)').Trim())</_ExistingGitHash>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(_GitHashFile)"
                     Lines="$(_GitHashContent)"
                     Overwrite="true"
                     Condition="'$(_ExistingGitHash)' != '$(_GitHashContent)'" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GitHashFile)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
Now let me analyze the two custom targets for incremental build issues.

**Target 1: `GenerateTimestamp`** (line 6-11)
- Runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` — every build
- Writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` to `build-timestamp.txt`
- **Problem**: No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, AND the content changes every time (contains current timestamp), so even if you added Inputs/Outputs, the file content would always change, causing CoreCompile to re-run since it's a new file in the intermediate output path.


- This is a classic incremental build killer: it writes a file that changes every build, which likely invalidates downstream targets.

**Target 2: `EmbedGitHash`** (line 13-23)
- Runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` — every build
- Executes `git rev-parse HEAD` every time
- Writes the git hash to `git-hash.g.cs` with `Overwrite="true"`
- Adds it to `<Compile>` items
- **Problem**: No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so this target runs unconditionally on every build. Even though the git hash typically doesn't change, the lack of incremental build tracking means it's re-executing the git command and regenerating the file unnecessarily.

Both targets are missing `Inputs` and `Outputs` attributes, which would let MSBuild skip them when their dependencies haven't changed. The `GenerateTimestamp` target is especially problematic since its output literally changes every second, forcing CoreCompile to rerun constantly. The `EmbedGitHash` target at least has stable output most of the time, but it's still invoking an external process on every single build without any caching mechanism.

I need to think through the actual impact here. The timestamp file is just a .txt file, not a compiled source file, so it shouldn't directly invalidate CoreCompile's inputs. But let me reconsider whether MSBuild is tracking this differently than I initially thought.

The real problem is with `EmbedGitHash` — it writes a `.g.cs` file that gets added to `<Compile>`, and since it always overwrites with `Overwrite="true"`, the file's timestamp changes even when the git hash hasn't. This forces CoreCompile to recompile everything downstream.

For `GenerateTimestamp`, the challenge is that there's no meaningful input to track — the whole point is to generate a new timestamp each time. I need to figure out whether this target should even run on every build or if it should be conditional. Outputs` based on the git hash itself. For `EmbedGitHash`, the real issue is that it always rewrites the file even when the hash hasn't changed, which invalidates downstream targets. The fix is to write to a temporary file first, compare it with the existing output, and only overwrite if the content actually differs—this way the file timestamp stays stable when the hash hasn't changed.

A simpler approach in MSBuild is to use `.git/HEAD` and the ref it points to as the `Inputs`, with the generated file as `Outputs`. This ensures the target only reruns when the git HEAD actually changes, avoiding unnecessary rebuilds. For the timestamp target, I need to add `Inputs` and `Outputs` attributes so it only regenerates when source files actually change. For the git hash embedding, `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` always touches the file even if nothing changed, so I should use `WriteOnlyWhenDifferent="true"` instead to avoid unnecessary rebuilds—this is simpler and more robust than trying to track git repository state through MSBuild inputs and outputs. For `GenerateTimestamp`, I'd add the project file and compiled sources as inputs with the timestamp file as output so it only regenerates when those change. For `EmbedGitHash`, the challenge is that we want it to run when the git hash changes, not just when the project file changes—so a practical workaround is writing to a temporary file first, then comparing its content to the actual embedded file and only updating if they differ, ensuring downstream targets don't unnecessarily recompile.

Writing the MSBuild target... I need to figure out the cleanest way to handle the git hash target so it doesn't unnecessarily touch the output file. The key insight is that the target needs to run every time to check if the hash changed, but I should only write to the file when the content actually differs. I'm considering using a property function to read the existing file and compare it before deciding whether to write, which would be simpler than creating temp files or using external scripts. Finishing the MSBuild target configuration...

The real culprit here is `GenerateTimestamp` — it has no `Inputs` or `Outputs` defined, so it always executes, and since it generates a new timestamp every run with `DateTime.Now`, the output file constantly changes, which completely breaks incremental compilation. That's the primary issue breaking the build system.

`EmbedGitHash` has a similar problem: no `Inputs`/`Outputs` means it always runs, and even when the git hash hasn't changed, `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` still updates the file's timestamp, causing CoreCompile to see it as modified and recompile everything. The fix is to add proper `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes to both targets — using the project file and compile items for `GenerateTimestamp`, and `.git/HEAD` plus the ref it points to for `EmbedGitHash`. But I need to check whether we're actually in a git repository first. Writing the MSBuild targets... Finishing the file inclusion and then realizing the issue: using the project file as the input means the git hash only regenerates when the project changes, not when commits happen. The real solution is to track `.git/HEAD` as the input instead, since that's what actually changes with new commits.

However, resolving git refs is complex, so I'm going to adopt the approach used by tools like GitVersion and MinVer: let the target run every time but only write the output file if the content actually changed, avoiding unnecessary downstream rebuilds.

For the timestamp target, I'll add inputs and outputs so it only runs when source files change, since the timestamp should reflect when the build was triggered by actual changes. For the git hash target, I'm setting it up to run unconditionally but write the file only when the hash differs, preventing CoreCompile from being invalidated unnecessarily.

Writing the MSBuild targets... Writing MSBuild configuration... I'm realizing that `BeforeTargets` will run the target before the specified target executes, regardless of whether that target is skipped. The simpler approach would be to add the Compile item as a static ItemGroup with a condition checking if the file exists, but that won't work on the first build since the file hasn't been generated yet. So the current approach of adding it dynamically inside the target body is actually the right solution here.

For the `EmbedGitHash` target, I should use `WriteLinesToFile` conditionally—only writing when the content has actually changed to avoid unnecessary timestamp updates. I can leverage MSBuild property functions like `$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText(...))` to read back the file content, but I need to account for the fact that `WriteLinesToFile` appends newlines, so I'll need to trim the comparison.

The XML escaping should work out correctly: `"` and `%3B` in the attribute get decoded to their actual characters (`"` and `;`) both when stored in the property and when written to the file, so the comparison between the property value and the file content should be reliable.

Now I'll lay out the analysis clearly and apply the straightforward fixes for both issues.

arguments.old_str:
<Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
      <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
    </Exec>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                     Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                     Overwrite="true" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Only regenerate the timestamp when source files or the project file change -->
  <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
          Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(Compile)"
          Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt"
                     Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())"
                     Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Always resolve the git hash, but only rewrite the file when it actually changes
       so the unchanged timestamp doesn't invalidate CoreCompile -->
  <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
      <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
    </Exec>
    <PropertyGroup>
      <_GitHashFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs</_GitHashFile>
      <_GitHashContent>namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }</_GitHashContent>
      <_ExistingGitHash Condition="Exists('$(_GitHashFile)')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('$(_GitHashFile)').Trim())</_ExistingGitHash>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(_GitHashFile)"
                     Lines="$(_GitHashContent)"
                     Overwrite="true"
                     Condition="'$(_ExistingGitHash)' != '$(_GitHashContent)'" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GitHashFile)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <!-- Only regenerate the timestamp when source files or the project file change -->
7.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
8.           Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(Compile)"
9.           Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt"
11.                      Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())"
12.                      Overwrite="true" />
13.   </Target>
14. 
15.   <!-- Always resolve the git hash, but only rewrite the file when it actually changes
16.        so the unchanged timestamp doesn't invalidate CoreCompile -->
17.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
18.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
19.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
20.     </Exec>

23.       <_GitHashContent>namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }</_GitHashContent>
24.       <_ExistingGitHash Condition="Exists('$(_GitHashFile)')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('$(_GitHashFile)').Trim())</_ExistingGitHash>
25.     </PropertyGroup>

diff --git a/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj b/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj
@@ -1,35 +1,35 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <!-- Only regenerate the timestamp when source files or the project file change -->
   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
           Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(Compile)"
           Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt"
                      Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())"
                      Overwrite="true" />
   </Target>
 
   <!-- Always resolve the git hash, but only rewrite the file when it actually changes
        so the unchanged timestamp doesn't invalidate CoreCompile -->
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <PropertyGroup>
       <_GitHashFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs</_GitHashFile>
       <_GitHashContent>namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }</_GitHashContent>
       <_ExistingGitHash Condition="Exists('$(_GitHashFile)')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('$(_GitHashFile)').Trim())</_ExistingGitHash>
     </PropertyGroup>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(_GitHashFile)"
                      Lines="$(_GitHashContent)"
                      Overwrite="true"
                      Condition="'$(_ExistingGitHash)' != '$(_GitHashContent)'" />
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Include="$(_GitHashFile)" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

result.content:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <!-- Only regenerate the timestamp when source files or the project file change -->
7.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
8.           Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(Compile)"
9.           Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt"
11.                      Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())"
12.                      Overwrite="true" />
13.   </Target>
14. 
15.   <!-- Always resolve the git hash, but only rewrite the file when it actually changes
16.        so the unchanged timestamp doesn't invalidate CoreCompile -->
17.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
18.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
19.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
20.     </Exec>
21.     <PropertyGroup>
22.       <_GitHashFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs</_GitHashFile>
23.       <_GitHashContent>namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }</_GitHashContent>
24.       <_ExistingGitHash Condition="Exists('$(_GitHashFile)')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('$(_GitHashFile)').Trim())</_ExistingGitHash>
25.     </PropertyGroup>
26.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(_GitHashFile)"
27.                      Lines="$(_GitHashContent)"
28.                      Overwrite="true"
29.                      Condition="'$(_ExistingGitHash)' != '$(_GitHashContent)'" />
30.     <ItemGroup>
31.       <Compile Include="$(_GitHashFile)" />
32.     </ItemGroup>
33.   </Target>
34. </Project>
35.
result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj b/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-0ccf4d1018fc4b3eb3633062e4ac39a0/IncrementalBroken.csproj
@@ -1,35 +1,35 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <!-- Only regenerate the timestamp when source files or the project file change -->
   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
           Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(Compile)"
           Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt"
                      Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())"
                      Overwrite="true" />
   </Target>
 
   <!-- Always resolve the git hash, but only rewrite the file when it actually changes
        so the unchanged timestamp doesn't invalidate CoreCompile -->
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <PropertyGroup>
       <_GitHashFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs</_GitHashFile>
       <_GitHashContent>namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }</_GitHashContent>
       <_ExistingGitHash Condition="Exists('$(_GitHashFile)')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('$(_GitHashFile)').Trim())</_ExistingGitHash>
     </PropertyGroup>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(_GitHashFile)"
                      Lines="$(_GitHashContent)"
                      Overwrite="true"
                      Condition="'$(_ExistingGitHash)' != '$(_GitHashContent)'" />
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Include="$(_GitHashFile)" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>
